title: Add throttle/debounce for move events in Mineflayer adapter
description:
## Problem
`bot.on('move', () => sendEvent('move', botPos()))` fires on every physics tick during pathfinding — potentially 20 times per second. Each fires a JSON payload across the WebSocket to C#, where `WorldStateProjector.ApplyMove()` creates a new `WorldState` record and writes a fact. This is already the status quo, but any new position-adjacent observations (entity positions, pathfinder position tracking) will compound the issue without rate control.

## Source
Council review DEF-PAPER-7 (Confidence: 95%). The research paper's Phase 1 proposal to add more movement telemetry compounds the existing issue without recommending any throttle.

## Requirements
1. Add a throttle/debounce to the existing `bot.on('move')` handler in `MineflayerAdapter/index.js`
2. Use a named constant at the top of the file: `const MOVE_EVENT_THROTTLE_MS = 250;` (4 events/sec max)
3. On each move tick, update an in-memory position buffer; only emit `sendEvent('move', ...)` when the throttle window has elapsed
4. Ensure the last position is always emitted when the bot stops moving (flush on `goal_reached`, `path_update`, or action completion)
5. Verify existing `MoveEvent` consumers in C# still work correctly with reduced event rate

## Design Notes
- Position accuracy requirement: ±1 block is acceptable for planning. 250ms throttle at walking speed (~4.3 m/s) gives ~1 block of movement uncertainty, which is within tolerance.
- The flush-on-stop pattern ensures the C# side always gets the final position.

## References
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` — `bot.on('move', ...)` handler, line ~250
- `Agent.Core/WorldStateProjector.cs` — `ApplyMove`
- `Agent.Core/Events/WorldEvents.cs` — `MoveEvent`
- Council review: DEF-PAPER-7
